# Storage and Backup Services

## LRZ Online Storage (NAS)

### Overview

The Leibniz Supercomputing Centre (LRZ) provides central Online Storage (NAS) for all TUM members. Every TUM member automatically receives personal storage space. Additionally, organizational units can request project storage for collaborative work.

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "NAS Service Architecture"
flowchart TB
    subgraph Clients["Client Access"]
        Win["Windows<br/>(SMB)"]
        Linux["Linux<br/>(NFS)"]
        Mac["macOS<br/>(SMB/NFS)"]
    end

    subgraph Access["Access Methods"]
        LAN["MWN Network"]
        VPN["VPN Connection"]
    end

    subgraph NAS["LRZ NAS Infrastructure"]
        FS["Fileserver"]
        LDAP["TUM LDAP Authentication"]
        Backup["Backup Services"]
    end

    Win --> LAN
    Linux --> LAN
    Mac --> LAN

    Win --> VPN
    Linux --> VPN
    Mac --> VPN

    LAN --> FS
    VPN --> FS

    FS --> LDAP
    FS --> Backup
```

### Storage Types

| Type | Description | Provisioning |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| **Personal Storage** | Automatic for every TUM member | Automatic with TUM account |
| **Project Storage** | For organizational units / teams | Request through IT Service Center |

### Storage Specifications

| Specification | Value |
|--------------|-------|
| **Protocols** | NFS, SMB (CIFS) |
| **Access** | MWN network + VPN |
| **Authentication** | TUM Central LDAP |
| **Backup** | Automatic via LRZ backup services |

### Access Protocols

#### NFS (Network File System)

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Best for** | Linux/Unix systems |
| **Features** | Native Unix permissions |
| **Performance** | Excellent for Linux workloads |
| **Configuration** | Mount points defined in `/etc/fstab` |

#### SMB/CIFS (Server Message Block)

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Best for** | Windows systems, macOS |
| **Features** | Windows-native sharing |
| **Performance** | Excellent for Windows workloads |
| **Configuration** | Mapped network drives |

### Access Control

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "NAS Access Control Flow"
flowchart LR
    User["User"]
    User --> |authenticates| LDAP["TUM Central LDAP"]
    LDAP --> |verifies| Groups["User Groups"]
    Groups --> |determines| Access["Access Permissions"]
    Access --> |grants/denies| NAS["NAS Shares"]
```

User and group management for NAS access:

- **Users**: Managed through TUM Central LDAP
- **Groups**: Created and managed via TUMonline
- **Permissions**: Set by administrators per share/directory

### Requesting NAS Storage

To request project storage for your organizational unit:

1. **Contact IT Service Center**: Email it-support@tum.de
2. **Define Requirements**:
   - Expected storage capacity
   - Number of users
   - Access patterns
   - Security requirements
3. **Provisioning**: LRZ sets up the storage
4. **Configuration**: Administrator configures user access

::: {.callout-tip}
## Consultation Available
The IT Service Center offers consultation for designing your storage setup. Contact it-support@tum.de for assistance.
:::

## Backup Services

### LRZ Backup

All NAS data is protected by LRZ backup services:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Backup Architecture"
flowchart TB
    subgraph Source["Source Data"]
        NAS["NAS Storage"]
    end

    subgraph Backup["LRZ Backup Infrastructure"]
        Agent["Backup Agent"]
        Server["Backup Server"]
        Storage["Backup Storage"]
    end

    NAS --> |automatic backup| Agent
    Agent --> Server
    Server --> Storage
```

### Backup Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Frequency** | Regular automatic backups |
| **Type** | Incremental (only changed files) |
| **Retention** | Multiple recovery points |
| **Verification** | Automatic integrity checks |
| **Storage** | Secure LRZ infrastructure |

### Data Restoration

#### When to Request Restoration

- Accidental file deletion
- File corruption
- Need to access previous version
- Recovery after system issues

#### Restoration Process

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Data Restoration Process"
sequenceDiagram
    participant Admin as Administrator
    participant ITSZ as IT Service Center
    participant Backup as LRZ Backup

    Admin->>ITSZ: Restoration Request<br/>(via email)
    Note over Admin,ITSZ: Include: Path, Date, Description

    ITSZ->>Backup: Locate backup version
    Backup-->>ITSZ: Identify available snapshots
    ITSZ->>Backup: Initiate restoration
    Backup-->>ITSZ: Restoration complete
    ITSZ-->>Admin: Notification + location
```

#### Requesting Data Restoration

1. **Email**: it-support@tum.de
2. **Subject**: Data Restoration Request - [Department Name]
3. **Include**:
   - Full path to the file(s) or directory
   - Target date (which backup to restore from)
   - Description of what needs restoration
4. **Timing**: Request as soon as possible after data loss

::: {.callout-important}
## Act Quickly
Request restoration as soon as possible. While LRZ maintains multiple recovery points, earlier contact ensures best restoration options.
:::

### Required Information for Restoration

| Information | Description | Example |
|-------------|-------------|---------|
| **Path** | Full file/directory path | `/nas/department/projects/data.csv` |
| **Date** | Backup date to restore from | `2025-01-15` |
| **Type** | File or directory | Directory with all contents |
| **Urgency** | Priority level | Standard / Urgent |

## Storage Best Practices

### Data Organization

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Recommended Folder Structure"
flowchart TB
    Root["Department NAS Root"]
    Root --> Projects["Projects/"]
    Root --> Users["Users/"]
    Root --> Shared["Shared/"]
    Root --> Archive["Archive/"]

    Projects --> P1["Project_A/"]
    Projects --> P2["Project_B/"]

    Users --> U1["Mueller/"]
    Users --> U2["Schmidt/"]

    Shared --> Templates["Templates/"]
    Shared --> Resources["Resources/"]
```

### Recommendations

| Practice | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| **Organized Structure** | Use logical folder hierarchy |
| **Clear Naming** | Use descriptive file and folder names |
| **Regular Cleanup** | Archive or delete obsolete data |
| **Access Control** | Apply appropriate permissions |
| **Documentation** | Document folder purposes |

### What NOT to Store

- Temporary/cache files
- Personally identifiable data (without approval)
- Licensed software installers (use official sources)
- Extremely large media files (consider alternatives)

## Monitoring and Quotas

### Storage Monitoring

Administrators should regularly monitor:

- Current storage usage
- Usage trends over time
- Large files and directories
- Inactive data candidates for archiving

### Quota Management

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Expansion** | Contact IT Service Center for additional needs |
| **Monitoring** | Regular usage reports available |
| **Alerts** | Notification before quota exceeded |

## Summary

| Service | Description | Contact |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| **Personal Storage** | Automatic for TUM members | Automatic |
| **Project Storage** | For organizational units | it-support@tum.de |
| **Access Management** | User/group permissions | TUMonline |
| **Data Restoration** | Backup recovery | it-support@tum.de |
| **Quota Increase** | Additional storage | it-support@tum.de |
